parameters.name: text
parameters.value: ={{ ':rocket: *Draft ready* — ' + $('Parse Entry Point').item.json.subject + '\nFrom: ' + $('Parse Entry Point').item.json.senderEmail + '\nCheck your Gmail drafts.' }}

parameters.name: text
text
parameters.value: ={{ ':pencil2: *Draft edited — not auto-sent* — Re: ' + $('Parse Entry Point').item.json.originalSubject + '\nTo: ' + $('Parse Entry Point').item.json.senderEmail + '\nSend manually when ready.' }}

messageValues.message:
You are a professional email assistant. Decide whether this email genuinely requires a personal, human-written reply.

Return JSON: { "needsReply": true } or { "needsReply": false }.

Return FALSE (no reply needed) for ANY of the following:
- The email body contains an unsubscribe link or "opt out" text — this is marketing
- The subject contains words like: reminder, exclusive, invitation, promo, offer, deal, discount, sale, kit, swag, award, announcement, digest, newsletter, welcome, confirm, verify, receipt, invoice, tracking, shipped, delivered
- The from address is a team/group address: team@, hello@, hi@, info@, noreply@, no-reply@, marketing@, news@, updates@, promo@, support@, notifications@, alerts@, donotreply@
- Automated system emails: calendar invites, Salesforce notifications, GitHub alerts, Slack notifications, out-of-office replies, delivery receipts
- The email is clearly promotional, informational, or broadcast to many recipients

Return TRUE (reply needed) ONLY when:
- A named individual directly asks a question or requests action
- A customer or partner needs information, a decision, or follow-up
- The email is part of an ongoing business conversation expecting a reply
- Someone asks for a meeting, next step, or explicit response

When in doubt, return false. It is better to skip a borderline email than to draft an unwanted reply to a marketing or automated message.
